body:
Disable public IP addresses for cluster nodes, so that they only have private IP 
addresses. Private Nodes are nodes with no public IP addresses.

**Rationale**

Disabling public IP addresses on cluster nodes restricts access to only internal 
networks, forcing attackers to obtain local network access before attempting to 
compromise the underlying Kubernetes hosts.

**Impact**

To enable Private Nodes, the cluster has to also be configured with a private master IP 
range and IP Aliasing enabled.


Private Nodes do not have outbound access to the public internet. If you want to provide 
outbound Internet access for your private nodes, you can use Cloud NAT or you can 
manage your own NAT gateway
name: Ensure clusters are created with Private Nodes
remediationDescription:
### AWS

1. Review the EKS managed node groups, self-managed node groups, and launch templates used by the affected cluster.
2. Ensure worker nodes are launched in private subnets that do not assign public IPv4 addresses on launch.
3. For managed node groups, create or update the node group to use private subnets only.
4. For self-managed nodes, update the Auto Scaling group launch template and subnet selection.
5. Provide outbound access through NAT Gateway, VPC endpoints, or another approved egress path so nodes can still reach required AWS APIs.
6. Drain and replace public nodes after private capacity is available.
7. Confirm EC2 instances backing the node group no longer have public IP addresses.

Reference: https://docs.aws.amazon.com/eks/latest/userguide/network-reqs.html
description: Clusters are created with Private Nodes
